mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.5 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull pressure that forces central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window, or returns are positive when both series accelerate.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of declining industrial production months during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive equity returns over the intermediate horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.0 percent triggers a liquidity drain that compresses equity valuation multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Sixty-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered months, or the threshold shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside accelerating GDP growth confirms broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when GDP growth decouples from housing activity, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month signals robust manufacturing demand that drives positive short-term equity returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Five-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of high-growth industrial production months during recovery regimes, or the signal fails t-test significance.
